serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Lychee & Lime Soda is one glass, approximately 350 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from similar sweetened beverages and fruit juices in HPB Foodo, as exact match for 'Lychee & Lime Soda' is not available. Values for lychee, lime, and soda water were combined and adjusted for typical beverage formulation.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated based on sweetened fruit-flavored soda (GI 60) from University of Sydney GI Database, considering the high sugar content from lychee syrup. Lychee fruit itself has a GI of 50, but the syrup form and added sugar increase the overall GI.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content from added sugars (syrup) and its association with increased blood pressure risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage due to its high sugar content, which is independently linked to increased blood pressure risk, regardless of low sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (12.0g/100g) and medium glycemic index (60) from rapidly absorbed liquid sugars.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar content and medium GI, which can cause rapid glucose spikes; opt for unsweetened alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High intake of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased risk of cardiovascular disease.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: To reduce cardiovascular disease risk, it is advisable to limit consumption of this sugar-sweetened beverage, as high sugar intake is linked to adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar intake can promote inflammatory pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage as its high sugar content may exacerbate inflammatory processes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee & Lime Soda is a refreshing, non-alcoholic beverage originating from Singapore, typically made with lychee puree or syrup, fresh lime juice, and soda water, often garnished with mint leaves. It offers a sweet and tangy flavor profile with a fizzy texture. Nutritionally, it is characterized by a high sugar content (12.0g/100g) and a medium glycemic index (60), primarily due to the lychee syrup. It is low in protein, fat, and dietary fiber. From a clinical perspective, its high sugar content makes it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Cardiovascular Disease, as it can contribute to blood sugar spikes and increased risk of these conditions. It is generally considered vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: Nutritional value of lychee lime soda

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from similar sweetened beverages and fruit components from HPB Foodo, as an exact match for 'Lychee & Lime Soda' was not available. GI is an analogous estimate.
notes: Potassium, calcium, iron, vitamin A, B vitamins, folate, vitamin D, E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are estimated based on typical fruit-based sweetened beverages and general food composition data, as specific values were not available in the primary source for this exact dish. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid profiles are not typically available for simple beverages of this type.
